name: Ace of Pentacles
short_upright: A solid, tangible offering — opportunity arrives in concrete form. New work, money, or material foundations ready to be planted.
short_reversed: An opportunity slipping through fingers, or arriving without roots. Scarcity thinking, poor groundwork, or a chance not taken seriously enough.

name: Two of Pentacles
short_upright: Holding two demands in motion at once and finding rhythm. Resources, commitments, or roles juggled with practiced flexibility.
short_reversed: The juggling act is faltering — too many balls, not enough hands. Priorities collide, finances wobble, and something is about to be dropped.

name: Three of Pentacles
short_upright: Skilled hands working in concert toward a shared build. Recognition for craft, and the productive meeting of expertise and vision.
short_reversed: A collaboration that has lost its tune — roles unclear, effort uneven. Quality slips when the team is out of sync or working past one another.

name: Four of Pentacles
short_upright: A tight grip on what has been earned, prizing security above all. Stability is real, but so is the fear that fuels the clutch.
short_reversed: The fist relaxing — for better or worse. Either generous release of control, or resources slipping away as hoarding finally cracks.

name: Five of Pentacles
short_upright: Walking past warmth in the cold, sure that no help is offered. Material lack compounds with the loneliness of believing you must endure it alone.
short_reversed: The thaw begins — work returns, debts ease, doors that seemed shut reopen. A turning point arrives, often through asking for the help that was always there.

name: Six of Pentacles
short_upright: Resources flowing where they are needed, measured and fair. The dignity of giving freely and the grace of receiving without shame.
short_reversed: Generosity bent by power — gifts with hooks, charity that humiliates. Debts mount, scales tip, and exchange stops feeling honest.

name: Seven of Pentacles
short_upright: Pausing on the hoe to weigh what has grown so far. Slow returns reward the patient, and the work of cultivation is honored.
short_reversed: The harvest is thin or the soil was wrong from the start. Frustration with slow progress tempts a hasty pivot before the real lesson lands.

name: Eight of Pentacles
short_upright: Head down, hands repeating the craft until the craft becomes second nature. The honest grind of a skill being refined one strike at a time.
short_reversed: Going through the motions, or chasing flawlessness at the cost of progress. Either shortcuts erode quality or perfectionism stalls the work entirely.

name: Nine of Pentacles
short_upright: Standing in a garden you tended yourself, the fruit fully your own. Quiet luxury earned through discipline, and the contentment of independence.
short_reversed: The garden is borrowed, the comfort hollow, or the bills outpace the show. Material reliance — on others or on appearances — masks a thinner foundation.

name: Ten of Pentacles
short_upright: Generations gathered under one roof, the wealth that outlasts a single life. Roots, lineage, and the security of belonging to something built to endure.
short_reversed: Cracks in the inheritance — disputes over money, estrangement, or structures that will not hold. What was meant to last is being tested or lost.

name: Page of Pentacles
short_upright: A student of the practical arts, eyes fixed on a chosen coin. Fresh ambition rooted in real plans — willing to learn, willing to start small.
short_reversed: Big ideas that never break ground, or study that drifts without aim. Potential goes unused while the next shiny thing distracts from the actual task.

name: Knight of Pentacles
short_upright: The slow horse that always finishes the field. Quiet, dependable progress — unglamorous, but deeply trustworthy and built to deliver.
short_reversed: Routine hardened into rut — the same furrow turned without question. Stubbornness, monotony, or grinding effort that no longer moves anything forward.

name: Queen of Pentacles
short_upright: A capable hand that tends garden, ledger, and loved ones with equal care. Generous, grounded competence — abundance shared from a stable center.
short_reversed: The cup runs dry from pouring it out for everyone else. Self-neglect, overextension at home or work, or care that has tipped into control.

name: King of Pentacles
short_upright: A builder who has turned skill and patience into a stable kingdom. Mature command of resources, generous in providing for what depends on him.
short_reversed: The crown weighs heavy with greed or pride — wealth used to dominate rather than steward. Stubborn attachment to status, money, or the way things have always been done.
